steps.title: Find the slant heights
steps.content:
The two faces with base $18$ use half the width, $4.5$, in the right triangle. The two faces with base $9$ use half the length, $9$.
$$\sqrt{4.5^2+12^2}=\sqrt{164.25}=\frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2}$$
$$\sqrt{9^2+12^2}=15$$

steps.title: Add the surface areas
steps.content:
Add the rectangular base area and the four triangular face areas.
$$18\cdot 9=162$$
$$2(\frac{1}{2}\cdot 18\cdot \frac{3\sqrt{73}}{2})=27\sqrt{73}$$
$$2(\frac{1}{2}\cdot 9\cdot 15)=135$$

steps.title: Compute the total
steps.content:
The total surface area is
$$162+27\sqrt{73}+135=297+27\sqrt{73}\sim 527.7$$
The answer is 527.7.
